content: Just as the language teaching definition of reflection frames it as a metacognitive practice that helps language learners correct past errors and deepen conceptual mastery by reviewing their own output, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this structured, pedagogy-aligned framework to design a graded reflection curriculum for improving autonomous agent reasoning. This would treat the LLM like a learning student rather than a static problem-solver, where incremental reflection tasks build long-term reasoning ability instead of only correcting single outputs.
